## reference · design system governance and rollout sequencing maturity
* A design system governance process is crucial for a healthy design system that stands the test of time.
* The process should include the following steps:
  + Step 1: Product teams use the design system to help design and build new work.
  + Step 2: Product teams reach out to the design system team if a component doesn't exist or doesn't fulfill requirements.
  + Step 3: Determine if new work is a "snowflake" (one-off component) or part of the design system.
  + Step 4: Prototype initial concept.
  + Step 5: Review initial concept.
  + Step 6: Formal design system design/dev & testing process.
  + Step 7: Final review with product team.
  + Step 8: Documentation and stage for release.
  + Step 9: New design system release.
  + Step 10: Product team adoption and QA process.
* The design system team should establish clear support protocol, guidelines for managing snowflakes, and a cadence for releasing design systems.
* The process should be tailored to the organization's specific needs.
* Testing should include:
  + Content
  + Accessibility
  + Cross-browser/device
  + Responsive
  + Functionality
  + Internal code review
  + Internal design review
  + Trial in applications
* The design system team should communicate new releases via all appropriate channels.
* The product team should test the new work and follow the support process to handle any questions or issues.
Sources: https://bradfrost.com/blog/post/a-design-system-governance-process/
